meaning: adj.地位低的,卑贱的,谦逊的adv.位置低下的,低声地,谦逊地
examples: First to colonize the barren land are the lowly lichens, surviving on bare rock.
This lowly variety is not the kind of imagination I would like readers to use. What a crime!
Although the study was conducted in a lowly fruit fly, the principles were very likely apply to many animals.
Chef's tables have been around for a century--they used to be where lowly staff chowed down.
Strasburg (1-5) hasn't won since opening day, April 1, against the lowly Miami Marlins.
